purpose: Naming convention patterns and folder taxonomy rules for the organize-files skill. The AI uses these to design a consistent naming scheme before renaming any files.

naming_principles.scannable: A person should understand the file's content from its name alone, without opening it.
naming_principles.sortable: Names should sort predictably — put the most important dimension first (date, project, or topic).
naming_principles.consistent: Every file in a collection must follow the same pattern. Mixed conventions are worse than a poor consistent one.
naming_principles.no_spaces: Use hyphens (-) or underscores (_) instead of spaces. Spaces break command-line tools and URLs.
naming_principles.lowercase_preferred: Lowercase is safer across operating systems and avoids case-sensitivity bugs.
naming_principles.no_special_chars: Avoid: / \ : * ? " < > | ( ) & @ ! # $ % ^ ~ ` — use only letters, numbers, hyphens, underscores, and dots.
naming_principles.no_trailing_versions: Avoid: report_final_FINAL_v3_USE_THIS.pdf — use semantic versions or dates instead.

date_format.placement: Date goes first when files need chronological sort (logs, reports, snapshots). Date goes last when project/topic sort is more important.

reports_and_documents.notes: Use 'draft', 'v2', 'final', 'approved' at end only when version state matters.

design_and_creative.notes: For multiple sizes, include resolution at end: icon_512px, icon_1024px.

code_and_scripts.notes: Code files follow language conventions (snake_case for Python, kebab-case for JS/web, PascalCase for classes). Do not add dates to code files — use git for versioning.

versions_and_drafts.scheme: Use explicit version labels, not 'final_final' or numbered copies

versions.draft: First working version, not for distribution
versions.final: The last version intended for use (use sparingly)

by_project.use_when: Files belong to distinct, named projects or clients

by_date.use_when: Files are time-series or archival in nature (logs, reports, invoices)

by_type.use_when: Mixed file types from many contexts, user needs fast lookup by type

by_topic.use_when: Research, learning, notes — retrieval by subject matters most

hybrid.use_when: Large collection with both type and project dimensions

depth_rules.shallow: Use 1–2 levels for collections of <50 files
depth_rules.deep: Use 3–4 levels for >500 files or complex multi-project archives
depth_rules.catchall_warning: A folder called 'misc', 'other', or 'stuff' is a sign the taxonomy needs more specificity.

zip_output_rules.include: All renamed files in the new folder structure
zip_output_rules.top_level: The zip root should contain the top-level folder, not scatter files at root level
